findings.description: The statement 'RDBMSのカラム型定義がEntityのバリデーションアノテーションに対応する（例: `CHAR(8)` → `@Length(min=8, max=8)`、`NVARCHAR(10)` → `@Length(max=10)`）' presents an explicit mapping rule that does not appear in the source. The source only shows the table and code side-by-side without stating any formal correspondence between column types and annotation parameters.
findings.source_evidence: Source shows the USER table (CHAR(8), NVARCHAR(10), NVARCHAR(100)) and UserEntity code with @Length annotations as an example, but never asserts a type-to-annotation mapping rule.

findings.description: The `User` class — the primary PascalCase class used in all code examples in s1 — is absent from the hints array.
findings.source_evidence: s1 code example defines and uses `public class User { ... }` throughout the section.
